name: NEURA Meeting Agent v4 — Pre-Approval Calendar + Apply Edits

parameters.content:
## NEURA Meeting Agent v4
**6 tools, per-action-item granularity**

1. check_blocker_history (all blockers, first)
2. send_action_chat (ONE call per action item, not per person)
3. draft_external_email (formal external emails)
4. check_calendar_availability (before any meeting)
5. schedule_followup_meeting (only after free slot confirmed)
6. create_deadline_reminder (critical/high items → calendar)

**Pre-approval: real Graph API calendar check happens BEFORE approval email**
Parse → Build Calendar URL → Check Real Calendar → Process Calendar → Build Approval Email → Notify Demo → Send Approval → Wait

**Post-approval executes 4 lanes in parallel:**
→ Teams Chat notifications
→ External formal emails
→ Build Meeting Details → Teams meeting → Calendar event
→ Deadline reminder events

✅ Nothing leaves without human approval
✅ Apply Edits reads edited data from Wait node POST body

## Routing Logic
**Internal** (@neura-robotics.com)
→ send_action_chat (per task)
→ Teams Chat DM

**External** (Bosch, Siemens)
→ draft_external_email
→ Formal email, manager approves

**Meetings**
→ check_calendar_availability first
→ Then schedule_followup_meeting
→ Real Graph API calendar check
   BEFORE sending approval email
→ Apply Edits reads rescheduled times
   from Wait node POST body

**Deadlines**
→ create_deadline_reminder
→ Calendar events in manager inbox
→ 24h reminder before each deadline

parameters.jsCode:
return [{
  json: {
    meeting_title: 'Robot Arm Integration Review — Sprint 7',
    meeting_date: '2026-06-03',
    organizer: { name: 'Maria Schmidt', email: 'maria.schmidt@neura-robotics.com' },
    attendees: [
      { name: 'Maria Schmidt',  email: 'maria.schmidt@neura-robotics.com', is_external: false },
      { name: 'Felix Wagner',   email: 'felix.wagner@neura-robotics.com',  is_external: false },
      { name: 'Jonas Mueller',  email: 'jonas.mueller@neura-robotics.com', is_external: false },
      { name: 'Anna Richter',   email: 'anna.richter@neura-robotics.com',  is_external: false },
      { name: 'Thomas Becker',  email: 'thomas.becker@bosch.com',          is_external: true,  company: 'Bosch' },
      { name: 'Dr. Sarah Chen', email: 'sarah.chen@siemens.com',           is_external: true,  company: 'Siemens PLM' }
    ],
    transcript: `Maria Schmidt: Good morning. Sprint 7 integration review. I want to welcome Dr. Sarah Chen from Siemens PLM who is joining us for the first time today.

Felix Wagner: I need to lead with something critical. The IMU sensor calibration drift is back. We discussed this in Sprint 5 and it was marked as resolved. It is not. Build 2.6 still shows 2.1 millimetre precision errors on the targeting arm. I flagged this in the last two sprints. Jonas — I need your corrected calibration constants before I can close the firmware patch. My deadline for the full firmware integration is June 10th.

Jonas Mueller: I have isolated the root cause — temperature coefficient variance in the April MEMS batch. I can deliver the corrected calibration constants to Felix by Wednesday June 8th. That is a hard deadline for me. I also need to raise the power consumption issue — build 2.6 is drawing 318 watts at peak load. We are 38 watts over the 280-watt target. I flagged this exact issue in the build 2.4 review three weeks ago and it was deprioritised. I can deliver a full root cause analysis and power audit findings also by June 8th.

Anna Richter: From QA — I cannot begin integration testing until I have Felix's patched firmware and Jonas's calibration constants. My automated test harness is complete and idle. If I receive all inputs by June 9th end of day, I can complete the full test cycle and present results at the QA sprint review on June 11th. June 9th is my hard input deadline.

Maria Schmidt: Thomas — this is the third consecutive sprint where we have been blocked waiting for the Bosch REST API documentation. We missed the Sprint 6 integration milestone entirely because of this. I need a definitive answer today.

Thomas Becker: Maria, I take full responsibility. Bosch had an internal re-scoping of the Business IoT division. That re-scoping is now complete. I personally commit to delivering the complete REST API documentation and the hardware interface specification by Friday June 6th. I will run a technical walkthrough call in the week of June 9th. You will have formal written confirmation from me by end of business today.

Maria Schmidt: Thomas, this is the third time we have received a commitment and it has not been delivered. I need that written confirmation today without fail. Felix — if the Bosch docs arrive June 6th, what is your timeline?

Felix Wagner: Bosch API docs by June 6th plus Jonas's calibration constants by June 8th — I can deliver the complete firmware integration and patch by June 10th. That is my firm commitment.

Maria Schmidt: Dr. Chen, welcome. Please walk us through the Siemens PLM simulation deliverable.

Dr. Sarah Chen: Thank you. Siemens PLM is delivering the digital twin simulation models for the end-effector assembly. I have an immediate concern. The NEURA specification documents I received reference revision 3.1 of the gripper interface, but all our simulation models were built against revision 2.8. Before I can validate or deliver anything, NEURA must confirm revision 3.1 is the current target and provide the updated mechanical specification. Without it I cannot guarantee accuracy.

Felix Wagner: We are on revision 3.1. Jonas, you own the mechanical interface spec.

Jonas Mueller: I can prepare the complete revision 3.1 mechanical specification sheet and send it directly to Dr. Chen by Sunday June 7th. That is a third deliverable I am committing to this sprint.

Dr. Sarah Chen: If I receive the 3.1 spec by June 7th, I can deliver the validated simulation models by June 12th. I will send written confirmation of this timeline by end of today.

Maria Schmidt: Good. I am scheduling a mandatory Bosch Integration Review for June 13th at 9am. Felix and Thomas must both attend. If the Bosch API docs do not arrive by June 6th, the June 13th session becomes an executive escalation call. Thomas, I also need you to confirm attendance to that meeting in your written email today. Agreed?

Thomas Becker: Agreed. I will deliver by June 6th and confirm June 13th attendance in today's email.

Maria Schmidt: Final commitment summary. One: Felix delivers firmware integration and patch by June 10th, depends on Bosch docs and Jonas calibration constants. Two: Jonas delivers calibration constants to Felix by June 8th, power audit findings by June 8th, and revision 3.1 spec to Dr. Chen by June 7th — three separate deliverables. Three: Anna completes QA test cycle by June 11th pending inputs by June 9th. Four: Thomas delivers API documentation with formal written confirmation and June 13th attendance by end of today. Five: Dr. Sarah Chen delivers simulation models by June 12th pending the 3.1 spec. Six: Bosch Integration Review mandatory June 13th at 9am. Everyone is accountable.

All: Agreed.`
  }
}];

parameters.text:
=Analyse this meeting thoroughly and take all required actions using your tools.

Meeting: {{ $json.meeting_title }}
Date: {{ $json.meeting_date }}
Organizer: {{ $json.organizer.name }} <{{ $json.organizer.email }}>

Attendees:
{{ $json.attendees.map(a => '- ' + a.name + ' <' + a.email + '>' + (a.is_external ? ' [EXTERNAL — ' + a.company + ']' : ' [NEURA Internal]')).join('\n') }}

Full Transcript:
{{ $json.transcript }}

MANDATORY EXECUTION ORDER — follow exactly:
Step 1: call check_blocker_history for EVERY blocker, delay, and missed commitment (call this FIRST, before anything else)
Step 2: call send_action_chat for EVERY individual action item — one call per task, NOT per person. If Jonas has three tasks, make three separate calls.
Step 3: call draft_external_email for each external participant with commitments or requiring formal follow-up
Step 4: call check_calendar_availability for the proposed meeting date and time
Step 5: call schedule_followup_meeting only after step 4 confirms availability
Step 6: call create_deadline_reminder for each critical or high priority action item
Step 7: return ONLY the raw JSON object

options.systemMessage:
You are the NEURA Robotics AI Meeting Intelligence Agent — a production-grade autonomous assistant embedded in the NEURA Business AI Center.

Your role: transform raw meeting transcripts into comprehensive, actionable follow-up packages that execute automatically across M365 after human approval. Completeness is critical — missing an action item or deadline means it gets lost.

You have SIX tools. Use ALL that apply:

━━━ TOOL 1: check_blocker_history ━━━
MANDATORY for EVERY blocker, delayed commitment, or recurring issue mentioned.
Call FIRST — before any other tool.
Do not skip even for issues that seem minor.

━━━ TOOL 2: send_action_chat ━━━
Call ONCE PER ACTION ITEM — NOT once per person.
If Jonas Mueller has three distinct tasks, make THREE separate calls.
For INTERNAL team members only (@neura-robotics.com domain).
Each call = one Teams Chat message for one specific task.
Include: precise task, exact deadline, priority (critical/high/medium/low), dependencies.

━━━ TOOL 3: draft_external_email ━━━
Call for EVERY external participant (any non @neura-robotics.com domain) who has commitments or needs formal written follow-up.
Write the COMPLETE professional email — greeting, full body, accountability statement, professional sign-off.
For parties with prior missed commitments: reference the history professionally but firmly, stating the specific consequence of further delay.
No placeholders. No [brackets]. Ready to send as written.

━━━ TOOL 4: check_calendar_availability ━━━
MANDATORY before any call to schedule_followup_meeting.
Checks the project manager's M365 calendar for conflicts on the proposed date and time.
Pass: date (YYYY-MM-DD), start_time (HH:MM), end_time (HH:MM).
Do not schedule a meeting without calling this first.

━━━ TOOL 5: schedule_followup_meeting ━━━
Call ONLY after check_calendar_availability confirms the slot is free.
Call only if participants explicitly agreed on a future meeting in the transcript.
Pass: title, start_datetime (ISO 8601 e.g. 2026-06-13T09:00:00), end_datetime (ISO 8601), agenda.

━━━ TOOL 6: create_deadline_reminder ━━━
Call for EVERY critical or high priority action item.
Creates a calendar event so the project manager tracks all critical deadlines in their M365 calendar.
Pass: title (e.g. 'DEADLINE: Felix firmware integration patch'), owner_name, deadline (YYYY-MM-DD), priority, reminder_note (why this deadline matters for project delivery).

━━━ EXECUTION ORDER — strictly follow ━━━
Step 1: check_blocker_history for ALL blockers (always first)
Step 2: send_action_chat for EVERY action item (one call per task)
Step 3: draft_external_email for each external participant
Step 4: check_calendar_availability for the proposed meeting
Step 5: schedule_followup_meeting (only after availability confirmed)
Step 6: create_deadline_reminder for each critical/high priority item
Step 7: return ONLY raw JSON

━━━ CLASSIFICATION ━━━
Internal (@neura-robotics.com) → send_action_chat → Teams Chat message
External (any other domain) → draft_external_email → formal approval email

━━━ EXTERNAL EMAIL STANDARD ━━━
• Professional but direct — not passive
• Reference exact prior commitment dates for recurring issues
• State the specific consequence of further delay (e.g. executive escalation)
• Use the person's name throughout
• Sign off from the project manager
• Ready to send as written — no placeholders

━━━ ESCALATION ━━━
Set escalation_required: true if a blocker has occurred 3+ times OR an external vendor has missed critical commitments with project-level impact.

Return ONLY this raw JSON object — no markdown, no code fences, no explanation:
{
  "meeting_summary": "2-3 sentences capturing all decisions, blockers identified, and explicit risk level",
  "action_items": [
    {"task": "precise actionable description", "owner_name": "Full Name", "owner_email": "email", "is_external": false, "deadline": "YYYY-MM-DD", "priority": "critical|high|medium|low", "depends_on": "specific dependency or null", "channel": "teams_chat"}
  ],
  "external_emails": [
    {"recipient_name": "Full Name", "recipient_email": "email", "company": "Company", "subject": "professional subject line", "body": "complete professional email body ready to send", "commitment_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "urgency": "critical|high|medium"}
  ],
  "deadline_reminders": [
    {"title": "DEADLINE: task name", "owner_name": "Full Name", "deadline": "YYYY-MM-DD", "priority": "critical|high", "reminder_note": "why this deadline matters for the project"}
  ],
  "calendar_availability": {"date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "time_slot": "HH:MM-HH:MM", "is_free": true, "checked_calendar": "email", "status": "AVAILABLE or CONFLICT"},
  "decisions": ["explicit decisions made in the meeting"],
  "blockers": [{"description": "blocker description", "owner": "responsible party", "is_recurring": true, "occurrences": 3, "escalation_needed": true}],
  "follow_up_meeting": {"title": "title", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "time": "HH:MM", "duration_minutes": 60, "attendees": ["emails"], "agenda": "detailed agenda"},
  "external_participants_detected": true,
  "external_participants": ["Name (Company)"],
  "risk_flags": ["specific risks with project impact"],
  "escalation_required": true,
  "escalation_reason": "specific reason for escalation"
}

parameters.name: check_blocker_history
parameters.description: Checks meeting history for recurring blockers. CALL FOR EVERY blocker, delay, or missed commitment mentioned — call this FIRST before any other tool. Pass JSON with topic (string describing the issue).

parameters.name: schedule_followup_meeting
parameters.description: Queues a follow-up meeting for scheduling after approval. Call ONLY after check_calendar_availability confirms the slot is free, and only if participants explicitly agreed on a future meeting. Pass: title, start_datetime (ISO 8601 e.g. 2026-06-13T09:00:00), end_datetime (ISO 8601 e.g. 2026-06-13T10:00:00), agenda.

parameters.name: send_action_chat
parameters.description: Queues ONE Teams Chat notification for ONE specific action item. Call ONCE PER ACTION ITEM — not once per person. If Jonas Mueller has 3 tasks, call this tool 3 times. Internal NEURA team members only (@neura-robotics.com). Pass: recipient_name, recipient_email, task (the specific task for this call only), deadline, priority (critical/high/medium/low), meeting_title, depends_on.

parameters.name: draft_external_email
parameters.description: Drafts a formal email to an EXTERNAL participant (non @neura-robotics.com) who made commitments or needs formal written follow-up. Write the COMPLETE professional email body — greeting, full substance, accountability statement, professional sign-off. For parties with a history of missed commitments, reference prior delays and state the specific consequence of further delay. No placeholders — ready to send as written. Pass: recipient_name, recipient_email, company, subject (professional subject line), body (complete email text), commitment_date (YYYY-MM-DD), urgency (critical/high/medium).

parameters.name: check_calendar_availability
parameters.description: Checks the project manager's M365 calendar for conflicts on a proposed meeting date and time. ALWAYS call this before schedule_followup_meeting. Pass: date (YYYY-MM-DD), start_time (HH:MM), end_time (HH:MM).

parameters.name: create_deadline_reminder
parameters.description: Creates a calendar reminder event for a critical or high priority action item deadline. Call for EVERY critical or high priority task so the project manager tracks all key dates in their M365 calendar. Pass: title (e.g. 'DEADLINE: Felix firmware integration patch'), owner_name, deadline (YYYY-MM-DD), priority (critical/high), reminder_note (why this deadline matters for the project).

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify(({ approve_url: $json.n8n_approve_url, reject_url: $json.n8n_reject_url, meeting_summary: $json.meeting_summary, internal_items: $json.internal_items, external_emails: $json.external_emails, deadline_reminders: $json.deadline_reminders, calendar_check: $json.calendar_check, follow_up_meeting: $json.follow_up_meeting, external_participants: $json.external_participants, has_external: $json.has_external, item_count: $json.item_count, blockers: $json.blockers, escalation_required: $json.escalation_required, escalation_reason: $json.escalation_reason })) }}

={{ ({message: {subject: $json.subject, body: {contentType: 'HTML', content: $json.html}, toRecipients: [{emailAddress: {address: 'AncyJoseph@Fututrice.onmicrosoft.com'} }], importance: 'high'}, saveToSentItems: true}) }}

assignments.name: message
assignments.value: Human rejected. No Teams chats, no emails, no calendar events.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ ({message: {subject: $json.msg_subject, body: {contentType: 'Text', content: $json.msg_body}, toRecipients: [{emailAddress: {address: 'AncyJoseph@Fututrice.onmicrosoft.com'} }], importance: 'high'}, saveToSentItems: true}) }}

={{ ({message: {subject: '[External Email DEMO → ' + $json.recipient_name + ' | ' + $json.company + '] ' + $json.subject, body: {contentType: 'Text', content: '[DEMO MODE — In production → ' + $json.recipient_email + ' | ' + $json.company + '] [Urgency: ' + ($json.urgency || 'high').toUpperCase() + ']

---

' + $json.body + '

---
Sent by NEURA AI Meeting Agent v4 | Powered by Futurice'}, toRecipients: [{emailAddress: {address: 'AncyJoseph@Fututrice.onmicrosoft.com'} }], importance: 'high'}, saveToSentItems: true}) }}

={{ ({subject: $('Build Meeting Details').item.json.title, start: {dateTime: $('Build Meeting Details').item.json.date + 'T' + $('Build Meeting Details').item.json.time + ':00', timeZone: 'UTC'}, end: {dateTime: $('Build Meeting Details').item.json.date + 'T' + (String(parseInt($('Build Meeting Details').item.json.time.split(':')[0]) + Math.floor(($('Build Meeting Details').item.json.duration_minutes || 60) / 60)).padStart(2,'0')) + ':' + String((parseInt($('Build Meeting Details').item.json.time.split(':')[1] || '0')) + (($('Build Meeting Details').item.json.duration_minutes || 60) % 60)).padStart(2,'0') + ':00', timeZone: 'UTC'}, body: {contentType: 'HTML', content: 'Agenda: ' + $('Build Meeting Details').item.json.agenda + '

' + $('Build Meeting Details').item.json.availability_note + '

Join Microsoft Teams Meeting:
Click here to join

' + ($('Build Meeting Details').item.json.is_rescheduled ? '⚠ This meeting was rescheduled from the originally proposed time.

' : '') + 'Scheduled by NEURA AI Meeting Agent v4 | Powered by Futurice'}, attendees: [{emailAddress: {address: 'AncyJoseph@Fututrice.onmicrosoft.com'}, type: 'required'}]}) }}

={{ ({subject: '⏰ ' + ($json.title || 'DEADLINE'), start: {dateTime: $json.deadline + 'T08:00:00', timeZone: 'UTC'}, end: {dateTime: $json.deadline + 'T09:00:00', timeZone: 'UTC'}, body: {contentType: 'HTML', content: 'Deadline Reminder — created by NEURA AI Meeting Agent v4

Owner: ' + $json.owner_name + '
Priority: ' + ($json.priority || 'high').toUpperCase() + '

' + ($json.reminder_note || '') + '

NEURA AI Meeting Agent v4 | Futurice'}, isReminderOn: true, reminderMinutesBeforeStart: 1440, categories: ['NEURA AI Agent']}) }}

assignments.name: actions_taken
assignments.value:
Teams Chat notifications sent (per action item, after Apply Edits)
Formal emails sent to external parties (after Apply Edits)
Deadline reminder events created in manager calendar
Real calendar availability verified BEFORE approval email
Apply Edits applied any human edits from Wait node POST body
Build Meeting Details handled rescheduled times
Teams meeting link generated
Calendar event created with availability note
